# Jacob (Jake) Waldo

> Personal portfolio website for Jacob Waldo — economics & data science student at USC, incoming Goldman Sachs Market Risk Intern, quantitative researcher, and artist.

## About

Jake is a junior at the University of Southern California (B.S. Economics & Data Science, graduating Dec 2026), previously at Chapman University (transferred 2024, 4.0 GPA). He holds a 3.97 GPA at USC and has passed the FRM® Part I exam (August 2025, 76th–100th percentile across all four domains).

He is an incoming Market Risk Intern at Goldman Sachs (Summer 2026, Dallas, TX) in the Market Risk Division.

## Research & Projects

- **Early-Exercise Premium for American Puts** (2025): Bloomberg Terminal options analysis using Monte Carlo simulation with GPU acceleration (26× speedup via CuPy). Analyzed EEP bounds, regression coefficients, and sensitivity across strike prices and expirations using put-call parity.

- **LLM Bias Detection Pipeline** (2025): LangGraph-powered pipeline investigating bias across GPT-5, GPT-5 Nano, Ollama, and Gemini models. MapReduce-style summarization reduces ~40-page document reviews from ~1 hour to under 5 minutes. Built in collaboration with Dr. James Alcala at USC.

- **College Federal Reserve Challenge** (Oct 2025): National FOMC simulation competition. Analyzed Core CPI (2.9%), Core PCE (3.1%), and labor market conditions. Team recommendation: hold Fed Funds Rate at 4.00–4.25%.

## Experience

- **Goldman Sachs** — Incoming Market Risk Intern, Market Risk Division, Dallas TX (Summer 2026)
- **USC Dornsife** — Supplemental Instruction Leader for ECON 317: Statistics for Economists (Aug 2025–Present); leads 3 sessions/week for ~100 students
- **USC** — Undergraduate Research Assistant with Dr. James Alcala investigating LLM bias (Jun 2025–Jan 2026)

## Skills & Credentials

- **Certification:** FRM® Part I — Passed August 2025, top quartile across all four exam domains
- **Programming:** Python, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, CuPy, SQL, Java, C++
- **Quantitative Finance:** Monte Carlo Simulation, Derivatives Valuation, Fixed Income, Market Risk, Options Pricing
- **Tools:** Bloomberg Terminal, Stata, Excel, LangGraph, Git, GPU Computing
- **Mathematics:** Probability Theory, Linear Algebra, Econometrics, Multivariable Calculus, Time Series

## Art & Illustration

Jake creates graphite, ink, and oil works exploring themes of psychology, the body, chronic stress, perfectionism, and social pressure. His featured 2025 piece — "The Body's Response to Chronic Anxiety and Stress" (ink, coffee wash, and acrylic on paper) — illustrates the biology of chronic stress including the HPA axis, allostatic load, and SAM system. A written breakdown explaining the scientific and emotional dimensions is available on the site.
